The handbook does not support a flat 20% pension match. Instead, it specifies a grade-based matching model where employer contributions range from 4% to 9% depending on the employee's grade (e.g., Grade 8: 4%, Grade 9–10: 6%, up to Grade 16–17: 9%). There is no maximum contribution, but the employee's salary must remain above the national minimum wage. The minimum employer match is 4% for all grades. Therefore, a new policy of always matching 20% would be a change from the current handbook rules. [CH0051]
